sourceMovie.title: J. Edgar
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto J. Edgar (2011) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Public Enemies
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If J. Edgar clicked for you, Public Enemies (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Snowden
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If J. Edgar clicked for you, Snowden (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after J. Edgar?
faq.answer: Try Public Enemies, Snowden, Black Mass—each shares DNA with J. Edgar while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is J. Edgar?
faq.answer: J. Edgar (2011) is best described as Drama, Crime, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is J. Edgar worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
